topic: hardware plugin naming and global RadioScale call standard
summary: The hardware plugin needs a proper project-fit name, must expose existing RadioScale functions as selectable targets, and requires a global standard for function/call naming across components.

requests.summary: Choose and implement an adequate project-consistent name for the new hardware plugin instead of leaving naming implicit or ad hoc.
constraints: name must fit the RadioScale project model
naming must be intentional and stable, not temporary
plugin remains installable as a plugin
acceptance: hardware plugin has a clear project-fit name
name is used consistently in repo-facing implementation and documentation
no temporary or placeholder naming remains

requests.summary: Make already existing RadioScale functions available as selectable targets/options in the hardware plugin where applicable.
constraints: existing RadioScale functions should already be available for selection where they exist
Codex must determine the exact repo-truth mapping of available functions per component
selection model must tolerate missing components safely
acceptance: hardware plugin exposes applicable existing RadioScale functions as selectable targets
selection options are based on real repo-truth functions rather than ad hoc guesses
missing components do not break runtime behavior

requests.summary: Define and apply a global RadioScale standard for function names, calls, and invocation targets across components.
constraints: this must be a global standard, not a hardware-local convention
component alignment is required before locking the standard
standard must cover naming of functions, calls, and invocation targets/events where relevant
standardization must reduce ambiguity between components
acceptance: one explicit global naming/call standard is defined
affected components are aligned to that standard or have a clear migration path
function/call naming is no longer component-specific drift
